name: A double bond trips the breaker
statement: Adding a second neutral-to-ground bond immediately trips the breaker, so the wiring mistake is self-protecting.
correction: Overcurrent breakers trip on overload or short circuit, not on a wiring mistake. A second N-G bond just splits the normal return current across two conductors; the total current is unchanged and well below the trip threshold. The breaker has no way to see the problem. The danger is invisible until a fault occurs or someone touches two bonded surfaces at different potentials.
